skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
stem: The average annual electricity cost for a certain home is $1,500. The homeowner plans to spend $18,000 to install a rooftop solar system. The homeowner estimates that the average annual electricity cost will then be $900. Which of the following inequalities can be solved to find t, the number of years after installation at which the total amount of electricity-cost savings will exceed the installation cost?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. The annual savings is 1,500 − 900 = 600 dollars per year. After t years, the total savings is (1,500 − 900)t. The savings exceeds the installation cost when 18,000 < (1,500 − 900)t.

Choices A, B, and C are incorrect; they reverse the inequality direction or fail to use the savings difference.
